title: Drink Dispenser Stand
summary: A cedar caddy that raises a beverage dispenser to pouring height with cutout handles. Fence-picket cheap, exterior-glued, a one-evening build.
description:
A drink dispenser is useless sitting flat on a table — you can't get a cup under the spigot. This little stand raises it to pouring height and gives you cutout handles to carry the whole thing, full jar and all, out to the party.

It's built from cedar fence pickets, which is the move for anything that lives outdoors and cheap: pickets are rot-resistant, already the right width, and a few dollars each. Because it holds a heavy glass jar of liquid, use a waterproof exterior glue and don't skimp on the corner supports — this is the one place a decor build turns structural. The inner 1x2 frame is what actually carries the weight; the picket panels are the skin.

The only shaped cut is the handles, drilled and jigsawed into the two shorter side panels. If you'd rather skip that, screw on a pair of hardware handles instead — either works.

tools.note: For the cutout handles. Skip it if you use hardware handles instead.
Or a circular saw. Square crosscuts on the pickets.
Or a staple gun. Glue-and-pin assembly.

materials.name: Cedar fence pickets, 72"
materials.note: Nominally 3-1/2" wide here. 1x4s work too, adjusting some measurements.

materials.name: Waterproof wood glue
materials.note: Exterior-rated — it holds a heavy jar of liquid outdoors.

steps.title: Build the inner frames
steps.body: Build the inner 1x2 frame first, using the corner supports and bottom supports — build two identical. This frame is what actually carries the weight of a full dispenser, so glue it well.

steps.title: Add the side panels
steps.body: Attach the picket side panels to the outsides of the two 1x2 frames with glue and 1" brad nails or staples. The panels skin the frame and hide the joinery.

steps.title: Add the end pieces
steps.body: Add the end pieces to join the two side assemblies into a box. Keep the top edges even so the dispenser sits level.

steps.title: Add the bottom and cut the handles
steps.body: Add the bottom slats to close the base. Then lay out the handle openings on the two shorter side panels, drill a starter hole, and cut them with a jigsaw — or skip the cutouts and screw on hardware handles. Sand any sharp edges.

images.alt: A cedar stand holding a glass beverage dispenser, with cutout handles
